title: D-SIG Signal
description: Distilled Signal Standard v0.5 — A vitality signal for distributed systems. Public Domain CC0.

dsig_version.description: D-SIG specification version used to produce this signal.

score.description: Normalised vitality score. 100 = optimal, 0 = total failure. Computed by implementation-defined weighting profile.

label.description: Semantic label. MUST be derived from score only (Rule 10). EXCELLENT=85-100, GOOD=60-84, DEGRADED=35-59, CRITICAL=0-34.

color.description: Visual code. MUST be consistent with label (GREEN=EXCELLENT, YELLOW=GOOD, ORANGE=DEGRADED, RED=CRITICAL).

trend.description: Temporal derivative of score over the observation window. Independent of label (Rule 10). CRITICAL_FALL overrides score for urgency assessment.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 production timestamp. Used for anti-replay validation and temporal analysis.

ttl.description: Signal validity in seconds. Recommended max: 600. After expiry, signal is STALE → treated as UNKNOWN / trend CRITICAL_FALL.

source_id.description: Stable producer identifier anchored on a persistent cryptographic key (e.g. Ed25519 fingerprint). MUST NOT be an ephemeral network address.

perspective.description: Producer perspective type. LOCAL=node inside observed network, CENTRAL=hub/aggregator, EXTERNAL=oracle outside the network.

source_sig.description: Ed25519 signature of the payload (base64). Required when trust_level >= 1. Proves content origin, not just channel identity.

source_pub.description: Fingerprint or base64 of the producer's Ed25519 public key. Enables signature verification without a central PKI.

trust_level.description: Declared trust level. 0=unsigned, 1=Ed25519 signed payload, 2=mTLS organisational CA, 3=delegated D-SIG CA.

baseline_cycles.description: Consecutive convergence cycles with reference perspectives. Producer-declared value is a synchronisation HINT only. Receiver MUST compute independently. A discontinuous jump is treated as Rule 7 (contradictory signal). 0=first emission or recent convergence break.

dimensions.description: Intermediate scores by dimension. Each entry SHOULD include score, timestamp, and ttl for individual freshness tracking.

ts.description: Timestamp of this dimension's last measurement.

ttl.description: Validity of this dimension's measurement in seconds.

flags.description: Optional flags for this dimension (e.g. STALE, CALIBRATING, UNREACHABLE).
Signal-level flags. Examples: CALIBRATING, TERRAIN_STALE, HUB_UNREACHABLE, INTERNET_UNREACHABLE.
